$comment: Windy Translate ops surface — Class C, but an INTERNAL support service (Node on loopback:8099, consumed by windy-pro; not directly grandma-facing). Two honest findings: (1) no /version endpoint → MF1 non-compliant (get_status is a gap); (2) no auth today → the ops shim's EPT passthrough is aspirational (the service currently ignores it, like a pre-token desktop app — add the EPT gate when/if exposed). Product API (/translate, /translate/batch, /detect) stays out per §2. get_capabilities → /languages: a translation service IS its language coverage.

get_health.note: rich: status + NLLB worker ready|loading + cache_size + uptime — doubles as status until /version exists.

get_status.note: NO /version endpoint — MF1 non-compliant; add the canonical {service,version,commit_sha,started_at,environment} shape.

get_capabilities.note: supported language list — the honest capability probe for a translator.

run_selftest.note: canary: translate a fixed phrase and verify output.

reset_to_defaults.note: holds the translation cache; clearing is a different, explicit action.

tools.name: get_health
tools.description: The FIRST call when translation is failing. Health of the Windy Translate service: whether the NLLB translation worker is ready or still loading, how big the cache is, and uptime. If the worker says 'loading', translations aren't ready yet.

tools.name: get_capabilities
tools.description: List the languages Windy Translate can actually translate between right now. Use this when a user's target language 'isn't working' — it may simply not be in the supported set.

tools.name: get_cache_stats
tools.description: Read Windy Translate's cache statistics — how many translations are cached and how often each language is hit. A diagnostic for 'is caching working' and which languages are in demand. Counts only, no translated content.
